outcome: Referral for or receipt of any lumbar spine imaging at last follow-up within 12 months
source_quote: Not reported in this paper. The study (PPACT trial) focuses on pain impact (PEGS), pain-related disability (RMDQ), satisfaction, and medication outcomes. No data on lumbar spine imaging referrals or receipt are reported.

outcome: Prescription or use of opioid medication at last follow-up within 12 months
source_quote: Continued long-term opioid therapy ... 12-month absolute risk difference, -0.010 [CI, -0.072 to 0.051]

outcome: Referral to surgeon or lumbar spine surgery at last follow-up within 12 months
source_quote: Not reported in this paper. The study (PPACT trial) is about CBT for chronic pain in opioid users and does not report on lumbar spine surgery referrals.

outcome: Pain at last follow-up within 12 months
source_quote: The mean difference in pain score between the two groups in the six-month follow-up was -2.269 (p-value 0.306). Mean pain score at 26 weeks: 13.6 (SDM&PR) and 16.3 (controls) (difference 2.7; p-value 0.385).
The mean difference in pain score between the two groups in the six-month follow-up was -2.269 (p-value 0.306). Pain (VAS) (0-100 mm): IV 13.6 (17.3), CO 16.3 (21.2) at 26 weeks.

This paper (Mason 2017) is a study protocol for the INDICATE-P feasibility study. It contains no results data. The paper states: 'no formal statistical analysis of the data between groups is planned' and 'no datasets were generated or analysed during the current study.'

This paper (Mason 2017) is a study protocol for the INDICATE-P feasibility study. It describes the Boston Carpal Tunnel Assessment Questionnaire SSS as a planned outcome measure but reports no results. The paper states: 'no datasets were generated or analysed during the current study.'
